_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-43R Tetracaine Ophthalmic (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA Optional.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-43R: Tetracaine Ophthalmic — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Chemical exposure to eye — to facilitate irrigation and reduce pain
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to tetracaine or ester-type local anesthetics
Known hypersensitivity to para-aminobenzoic acid (PABA) derivatives
precautions: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has approved tetracaine ophthalmic for EMS use
Instruct patient NOT to rub eyes after administration — patient will not feel pain and may inadvertently cause corneal injury
Topical anesthetic use does not replace need for thorough eye irrigation in chemical exposure
Anesthetic effect is temporary — patient will have return of pain once effect wears off (expect at hospital)
expected_effects: Allows patient to keep eye open for examination and irrigation
side_effects: Transient stinging or burning on instillation (resolves within seconds)
Corneal toxicity with repeated administration (limit to protocol-specified doses)
notes: Standard dose: 1-2 drops per affected eye; may repeat every 5 minutes up to maximum 5 doses per eye
Primarily used to facilitate eye irrigation after chemical exposure
After administration: protect eye from accidental self-injury (patient cannot feel pain)
MCA Optional — only if MCA has authorized

dosing.population: All (≥1 yr) — chemical exposure / eye irrigation
dosing.dose: 1-2 drops per affected eye; may repeat every 5 minutes; maximum 5 doses per eye
dosing.notes: MCA Optional; instruct patient not to rub eyes; use to facilitate irrigation
